parsed_review.summary: CP00-434 (RP14 Partner Settlement, Risk C, 150 units, range RP14.P03.M04.S16-RP14.P04.M02.S11) passes independent read-only review with zero findings. The pack-level production_ready claim is honest and conservative: manifest and command-evidence both record production_ready=false / runtime_ready=false with status "implementation_and_plan_gates_passed_pending_final_closeout"; only the contract-level production_ready_candidate is true, gated on coverage.valid && slice.valid. Deliverable distribution (implementation 60, contract 26, security_audit 18, test 15, hermes_evidence 5, claude_review 8, ui 18) and micro-title row counts reconcile to 150 across registry CP434_REQUIREMENTS, validators coverage checks, manifest delivery_counts, and the test suite. No-write/no-real-data boundaries are enforced in freezeCp434Result and the NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION (synthetic_only=true, no_real_data=true, writes_product_state=false, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false, executes_*_runtime=false, loads_real_fixture_data=false), and command-evidence confirms no_real_data=true with all 27 validation commands (full prompt suite) exiting 0. Authority boundary is intact: claude_role=read_only_independent_reviewer, claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true across registry, service, validators, and contract. Hermes (gate H14) is used only for deterministic control-plane review-packet/receipt validation, not embedded in product runtime. CP178+ review hardening holds: auth preflight passed with valid 1430-byte JSON result (not 0-byte, not fenced, not tool-call-shaped, no missing fields), readiness ready=true, and invalid_attempts_preserved is empty. Unrelated dirty files (the v2.0 docx, workbook/* artifacts, and docs/ldip-integration/.../c-ldip-01-invalid-attempt-01.json) are correctly classified out of pack scope and are not treated as this pack's review evidence. Post-review gated commands (closeout-pack:review/normalize/receipt:validate, closeout-pack-plan:generate, closeout-pack:validate, git diff --check --cached) are appropriately deferred until after an accepted review. As read-only reviewer I do not grant final approval or close the enterprise trust claim; this verdict supports closeout but human final approval remains required.
